description: End-to-end test deck for Mermaid diagram rendering pipeline validation. 10 slides covering DALP overview with 4 appendix Mermaid diagrams.

slides.title: What is DALP
slides.body:
DALP (Digital Asset Lifecycle Platform) is a complete platform for issuing, managing, and servicing tokenized financial instruments.

One contract type (DALPAsset) represents any financial instrument. Token features and compliance modules are runtime-pluggable building blocks, not compiled-in at deployment. Reconfiguration happens without redeployment.

Seven out-of-the-box asset templates: bonds, deposits, stablecoins, equity, funds, real estate, and precious metals. Any free-form asset type can be created beyond these starting points.

slides.title: Asset Lifecycle Management
slides.step1_body: Define asset parameters, compliance rules, and token features
slides.step2_body: Deploy smart contracts, mint tokens, distribute to investors
slides.step3_body: Ongoing coupon payments, corporate actions, compliance monitoring
slides.step4_body: Atomic DvP settlement, T+0 transfer, cross-chain operations
slides.step5_body: Maturity redemption, buyback, burn, and final settlement

slides.title: Compliance as Code
slides.body:
Every action on DALP passes through a layered compliance gate before touching on-chain state.

12 compliance modules covering geographic restrictions, identity access control, claim-based verification, supply and investor limits, time-based rules, and transfer controls.

7 regulatory templates out of the box: MiCA EU Standard, Reg D 506(b), Reg D 506(c), MAS Singapore, UK FCA Securities, Japan FSA Crypto, and Reg CF Crowdfunding.

Compliance modules are runtime-pluggable. Add, remove, or reconfigure without redeploying the smart contract.

slides.title: Integration Capabilities
slides.body:
DALP integrates with existing custodians, core banking systems, and payment rails via standard APIs.

Inbound: CRM, OMS, ERP, treasury systems, and external KYC providers connect through the DALP API gateway.

Outbound: settlement rails, CSD networks, and T+0 DvP infrastructure receive transaction data and confirmations.
slides.body_right:
Supported integration patterns:

REST APIs for programmatic access
Webhook-based event notifications
Batch import/export for bulk operations
SDK libraries for Node.js, Python, and Java
CLI tools for DevOps automation

All integrations are authenticated, rate-limited, and fully audited.

slides.title: Value Proposition
slides.item1:
One Contract, Any Instrument
A single DALPAsset contract supports bonds, equity, funds, stablecoins, deposits, real estate, and precious metals with runtime-configurable features.
slides.item2:
Features as Building Blocks
11 token features and 12 compliance modules snap together at runtime. No redeployment needed to add fee structures, voting power, or new compliance rules.
slides.item3:
Deploy in Weeks
Full lifecycle management from issuance to servicing. Seven asset templates provide proven starting points. Configure, deploy, and go live.
slides.item4:
Institutional Controls
Maker-checker approvals, RBAC/ABAC, HSM-compatible key management, and formal recovery procedures built into the execution layer.

slides.title: Use Cases
slides.item1:
Tokenized Bonds
Fixed-income instruments with automated coupon payments and maturity redemption
slides.item2:
Tokenized Deposits
Bank deposits on-chain with full regulatory compliance and instant settlement
slides.item3:
Stablecoins
Regulated stablecoin issuance with reserve management and compliance controls
slides.item4:
Fund Tokenization
Investment fund units with automated NAV calculations and distributions
slides.item5:
Real Estate
Fractional property ownership with rental yield distribution and secondary trading
slides.item6:
Precious Metals
Gold and silver-backed tokens with vault custody integration and physical redemption

slides.title: Appendix
slides.subtitle: Architecture and process diagrams for technical reference

slides.title: DALP Platform Architecture Layers
slides.body: DALP is structured in four distinct layers: channel layer (investor portals, REST APIs, operator console), business services (asset lifecycle, compliance, identity), execution layer (orchestration engine, maker-checker, event bus), and blockchain layer (EVM smart contracts, token standards, on-chain state).

slides.title: Compliance Check Sequence
slides.body: Every action passes through a layered compliance gate: identity check (DID and KYC status), whitelist check (approved register), policy engine (transaction rules and jurisdictional constraints), and limit check (holding caps and velocity limits). Blocked actions are rejected with a logged reason before any transaction is submitted.

slides.title: Digital Asset Lifecycle
slides.body: From initial configuration through issuance, distribution, ongoing servicing, atomic DvP settlement, and final redemption.

slides.title: Integration Topology
slides.body: DALP acts as an integration hub between enterprise back-office systems and the execution layer of smart contracts and custodians. Outputs connect to settlement rails, CSD networks, and T+0 DvP infrastructure.
